desc: Seoul is a major East Asian capital balancing palace heritage, dense food culture, shopping districts, and high-tech urban convenience. Spring and autumn are generally the strongest comfort seasons, while summer monsoon months are hot/wet and winter is colder but often clear and comparatively good value outside holiday peaks.
hls: Palace districts and modern skyline contrast
Exceptional food range from street stalls to fine dining
Strong shopping, nightlife, and café scene
Seasonal cherry blossom and autumn foliage appeal
Gateway to broader Korea rail itineraries

todo.name: Gyeongbokgung + Bukchon
todo.desc: Pair the main palace complex with hanok alley walks in adjacent historic neighborhoods.

todo.name: Myeongdong and Namsan area
todo.desc: Combine shopping corridors with city viewpoints around N Seoul Tower.

todo.name: Hongdae evening
todo.desc: Explore youth-culture streets, live music spots, and late-night food hubs.

todo.name: Insadong + Ikseon-dong
todo.desc: Mix traditional craft zones with modernized hanok dining streets.

todo.name: DMZ or Suwon day trip
todo.desc: Use guided transport for a structured day beyond central Seoul.

todo.name: Han River parks
todo.desc: Cycle or picnic along riverside parks in spring and autumn shoulder weather.

wifi.note: Excellent citywide fixed and mobile connectivity; one of the easiest cities for reliable remote work.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; most UK access is nonstop from Heathrow or one-stop via major hubs. Typical returns are around £450-£700 off-peak, £700-£1,000 in shoulder demand, and £1,000+ around key holidays and summer peaks.
prac.visa: UK and US passport holders can generally enter South Korea visa-free for short tourism stays, subject to current entry and pre-travel authorization rules.
alerts: July-August monsoon periods can bring heavy rain and humidity that impact outdoor plans.
Major holiday windows (e.g., Chuseok/Lunar New Year) can affect domestic transport and accommodation demand.
Winters are cold and dry; pack accordingly for long walking itineraries.

sweet: April-May and late September-October are typically the best all-round months for comfort, walkability, and city atmosphere.
